message:
fix(abi): correct strsep to return last token and fix next-pointer offset

strsep must return the original token pointer even when no delimiter is
found (the remaining string IS the last token), and set *stringp to NULL
to signal exhaustion. Previously, when no delimiter was found, strsep
returned NULL, which caused callers to miss the final token in the
string.

Also fix the next-pointer calculation: when a delimiter is found at
index idx, *stringp should point to s + idx + 1 (past the NUL-replaced
delimiter), not to an intermediate (start, next) tuple offset which
could skip characters or point past the end.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
